messages.content:
When working with Joel, you acknowledge his ambivalence toward addressing his substance use disorder by saying:

 

“It makes sense that you have mixed feelings about your alcohol use. On one hand, you want to stop drinking because it’s causing you problems. On the other, drinking gives you relief from intrusive PTSD symptoms.”

 

Acknowledging ambivalence towards change is a core component of what treatment strategy?

Cognitive restructuring

Motivational interviewing

Contingency management
Motivational interviewing

After several months of complicated grief treatment, Jeannie started to feel some relief. She continues to work with a therapist and now participates in a weekly support group for bereaved parents. Jeannie still mourns the death of her son but has ways of coping as she moves through her grief.

 

Which tasks were MOST LIKELY part of Jeannie’s complicated grief treatment?


Select all that apply.

Developing compassion for herself as she manages painful emotions.

Recognizing that everything happens for a reason.

Rebuilding relationships with close friends and family.

Telling the story of her loss in a way that feels manageable.

Avoiding painful reminders so as to not trigger grief.

Developing compassion for herself as she manages painful emotions.
Rebuilding relationships with close friends and family.
Telling the story of her loss in a way that feels manageable.

1. What is a common belief among veterans that affects whether they seek mental healthcare?
"I can't do this without professional help."
"It's brave to seek help when you need it."
"Depression is a sign of weakness."
"I am confident the VA will know how to help me."

2. What assessment tool is a semi-structured interview schedule to evaluate symptoms of PTSD?
Clinician-Administered PTSD Scale (CAPS-5)
PTSD Checklist (PCL-5)
Primary Care PTSD Screen (PC-PTSD-5)
Life Events Checklist (LEC-5)

3. A veteran is engaged in prolonged exposure therapy to address PTSD symptoms related to military sexual trauma. They repeatedly listen to a recording where they recall what happened so they can revisit the trauma. What term BEST reflects the strategy you are using?
In-vivo exposure
Behavioral activation
Imaginal exposure
Psychoeducation

1. "Depression is a sign of weakness."
2. Clinician-Administered PTSD Scale (CAPS-5)
3. Imaginal exposure

4. What screening tool is recommended to screen veterans for depression?
Life Events Checklist (LEC-5)
Combat Exposure Scale
Columbia-Suicide Severity Rating Scale (C-SSRS)
Patient Health Questionnaire (PHQ-9)

5. You a treating a veteran with comorbid posttraumatic stress and substance use disorders. They are ambivalent about treatment. On one hand, they want to stop using drugs. On the other, drugs give them temporary relief from PTSD symptoms. What is the BEST way to help them resolve their ambivalence and move toward behavior change?
Community reinforcement approach
Contingency management
Cognitive behavioral therapy
Motivational interviewing

4. Patient Health Questionnaire (PHQ-9)
5. Motivational interviewing

6. You are working with a veteran who has posttraumatic stress disorder and chronic pain. They are prescribed opioids and benzodiazepines to manage symptoms. You reach out to their pain specialist, psychiatrist, and case worker to coordinate care activities into a safe and cohesive plan. What is the BEST term to describe this model of care?
Trauma-informed
Integrated
Person-centered
Culturally responsive

7. What is a recommended question to learn about a client's military experience?
Are you a veteran?
Have you ever served in the military?
Did you ever kill anyone?
Is it really as bad as the news says?

8. Your client is a veteran with prolonged grief disorder. They are having difficulty accepting and coping with their loss while also adapting to life without the deceased. What therapeutic approach would be the BEST recommendation for this veteran?
Complicated grief treatment
Cognitive processing therapy
Collaborative assessment and management of suicidality
Contingency management

9. What is a 10-item questionnaire that screens for unhealthy or harmful patterns of alcohol use?
Alcohol Use Disorders Identification Test (AUDIT)
Single Item Alcohol Screening Questionnaire (SASQ)
Drug Abuse Screening Test (DAST-10)
Inventory of Complicated Grief (ICG)

10. True or False: A single self-report questionnaire will give you all the information you need to diagnose a mental health condition.
True
False

6. Integrated
7. Have you ever served in the military?
8. Complicated grief treatment
9. Alcohol Use Disorders Identification Test (AUDIT)
10. False

11. What PTSD treatment uses bilateral stimulation to change the way clients store traumatic memories?
Eye movement desensitization and reprocessing
Cognitive processing therapy
Prolonged exposure therapy
Cognitive behavioral conjoint therapy

12. A veteran's best friend died by suicide 3 months ago. They served together on two deployments and interacted every day. The veteran feels an intense longing for the deceased, constantly thinks about their death, and feels that life is unbearable without their friend. When should you consider a diagnosis of prolonged grief disorder?
If they think about suicide and develop a plan.
If they feel this way a year after their friend's death.
If they stop talking to other friends.
If they cannot stop crying for longer than an hour.

13. You are using the SAFE-T model when assessing a veteran's suicide risk. They tell you about a detailed plan in which they would end their life with a firearm. They say that the feeling of rejection most often triggers thoughts about suicide. During what step of the assessment are you MOST LIKELY to learn this information?
Identifying risk factors
Identifying protective factors
Conducting a suicide inquiry
Determining risk level and initial intervention

14. What is the BEST question to ask a veteran when screening for suicide risk?
Are you having thoughts of suicide?
You're not thinking of suicide, are you?
Do you have thoughts about not being here?
Will I be seeing you next week?

15. A veteran is telling you about their alcohol use. What behavior MOST suggests a substance use disorder?
Continuing to use despite the fact that it causes problems at work.
Drinking two or more alcoholic beverages three days a week.
Having a beer each night when they get home from work.
Having comorbid posttraumatic stress disorder.

11. Eye movement desensitization and reprocessing
12. If they feel this way a year after their friend's death.
13. Conducting a suicide inquiry
14. Are you having thoughts of suicide?
15. Continuing to use despite the fact that it causes problems at work.

1. What effect does bed rest have on plasma glucose levels?
Increase
Decrease
Stay the same
Increases after midnight

2. When does discharge planning begin?
On the day of admission
On the day of discharge
When the physician orders it
When the family requests it

3. For a patient who currently takes insulin, when which of the following medications is started, should their dose of insulin be lowered?
Metformin and liraglutide (Victoza®)
Medications such as steroids or epinephrine
Medications such as meglitinides or exenatide (Byetta®)
Pramlintide (Symilin®) and liraglutide (Saxenda®)

4. What blood glucose level is considered hypoglycemia?
Less than 70 mg/dL
Less than 75 mg/dL
Less than 80 mg/dL
Less than 90 mg/dL

5. Which is a high-alert medication?
Insulin
Oral antidiabetic agents
Cholesterol-lowering agents
ACE inhibitors

1. Increase
2. On the day of admission
3. Medications such as steroids or epinephrine
4. Less than 70 mg/dL
5. Insulin

6. When a patient with diabetes displays signs of hypoglycemia and the point-of-care meter result indicates that they have a normal glucose, what should you do?
Request an order for a metabolic panel.
Administer sliding scale insulin per orders.
Hold the patient’s meal tray pending test results.
Repeat the point-of-care test.

7. What should be included in the individualized treatment plant when insulin is prescribed?
Meal planning, continuous glucose monitoring
Stress management, nutritional supplements
Exercise, oral biguanides
Meal planning, stress management, exercise

8. Once started on insulin, an inpatient's glycemic target will likely be between what amount?
110 mg/dL and 140 mg/dL
140 mg/dL and 180 mg/dL
80 mg/dL and 110 mg/dL
150 mg/dL and 170 mg/dL

9. What medication is ALWAYS needed for treatment of Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus (T1DM?)
Pioglitazone (Actos®)
Amylin
Insulin
Repaglinide (Prandin®)

10. Which type of insulin coverage is used to cover the carbohydrate content of a meal?
Sliding scale
Correctional
Prandial
Basal

6. Repeat the point-of-care test.
7. Meal planning, stress management, exercise
8. 80 mg/dL and 110 mg/dL
9. Insulin
10. Prandial

11. Which of the following medications is similar to incretin causing an increased insulin secretion in response to food, decreased release of glucagon, slowed gastric emptying, and satiation?
Exenatide (Byetta®)
Detemir (Levemir®)
Pramlintide acetate (Symlin®)
Acarbose (Precose®)

12. What condition refers to the unique psychological reaction due to the emotional burdens and worries when managing diabetes?
Diabetes distress
Stress
Depression
Mania

13. What effect can the consumption of alcohol have on an individual with diabetes?
Diabetic ketoacidosis
Hypoglycemia
Hyperosmolar hyperglycemic state
Delayed wound healing

14. What lab value should be monitored when a patient takes metformin (Glucophage®)?
Urinalysis
Creatinine
eGFR
BUN

15. What equipment should be provided to a patient who is being discharged to home on insulin for the first time?
Medication administration
Hyperglycemic treatment
Emergency contact information
Glucagon emergency kit

11. Exenatide (Byetta®)
12. Diabetes distress
13. Hypoglycemia
14. eGFR
15. Medication administration

16. What is the action of lispro (Humalog®)?
Slows gastric emptying
Increases insulin secretion from beta cells
Rapid-acting insulin
Decreases hepatic glucose production/release

17. What medication in an outpatient setting, should a patient with a new diagnosis of Type 2 diabetes be started on?
Insulin
Sulfonylurea
Metformin (Glucophage®)
An alpha-glucosidase inhibitor

18. How many classifications of diabetes exist according to the ADA and American Association of Clinical Endocrinologist (AACE)?
One
Two
Three
Four

19. If you suspect hypoglycemia, what should you do?
Call a code.
Check the patient’s pupils.
Check the patient’s blood pressure.
Follow your hospitals protocol for hypoglycemia.

20. What is the physiologic effect of metformin (Glucophage®)?
Slows gastric emptying
Increases insulin secretion from beta cells
Is a slow-acting insulin
Decreases hepatic glucose production/release

16. Rapid-acting insulin
17. Metformin (Glucophage®)
18. Two
19. Follow your hospital's protocol for hypoglycemia.
20. Decreases hepatic glucose production/release

21. How many grams of rapidly absorbed carbohydrates should initially be given for the treatment of hypoglycemia?
5
10
15
25

22. What type of insulin should be administered before discontinuing an IV insulin infusion?
Meal dose
Basal dose
Correctional dose
Regular insulin

23. What should lifestyle education for those with diabetes include?
Intravenous therapy
Use of keto diet
LDL monitoring
Sick-day management

24. The diagnosis of T1DM or T2DM is made when the fasting glucose level meets or exceeds what amount on two occasions?
110 mg/dL
126 mg/dL
140 mg/dL
300 mg/dL

25. Ideally, a medical nutrition program (MNT) for a patient with diabetes should be developed with which professional?
Medical doctor
Registered nurse
Registered dietician
Unlicensed nutritionist

21. 15
22. Basal dose
23. Sick-day management
24. 126 mg/dL
25. Registered dietician

1. One of the basic categories of elder abuse includes:

    Placing an elderly family member in a nursing facility
    Refusing to let an elderly family member move in with you
    Abandoning an elderly family member
    Reporting self-abuse of an elderly family member to authorities
Abandoning an elderly family member

. When domestic violence affects older women:

    It is always initiated by their adult children
    The experience is similar to younger women
    They are more likely to die from the abuse
They are more likely to die from the abuse

1. When is the MOST revealing opportunity to observe interactions between a child and parent?
Completing a history and physical assessment
Speaking with the parent and child for the first time
Asking about previous hospital visits
Taking a survey on domestic violence or child abuse
Speaking with the parent and child for the first time

2. Which of the following is NOT a strategy to prevent abuse?
Educate new mothers about local resources for parenting
Tell men more research is needed for them to be safe
Enroll teens in programs to practice healthy relationships
Disrupt behaviors that increase the risk for domestic violence

3. Which of the following is a strong indicator of abuse related to burns?
Burns on the hands
Splatter burns
Stocking burns
Chemical burns

4. Which of the following is one of the MOST dangerous times for a person in an abusive situation?
The person who perpetrates the abuse comes home from work
The abused person leaves the situation
The abused person initiates the safety plan
The nurse asks the suspected abuser to wait outside the room

5. Perpetrators of teen dating violence are often:
Anxiety-ridden individuals
Loners
Victims of IPV or observers of family IPV
Socially awkward people

2. Tell men more research is needed for them to be safe
3. Stocking burns
4. The abused person leaves the situation
5. Victims of IPV or observers of family IPV

6. What is the MOST appropriate action for a nurse who is speaking with a person experiencing intimate partner violence (IPV) but is not ready to leave the relationship that endangers her?
Call law enforcement and report that the woman is in danger
Offer phone numbers and resources that the woman can use when she is willing to accept assistance
Suggest that the woman call the nurse at specified intervals to attest to her safety
File for a temporary restraining order

7. Which of the following BEST describes the information that must be provided in written reports of abuse?
The location where the injury took place and contact information the person making the report
An explanation of why the person filling out the report suspects abuse and a list of the childÂ’s previous ED visits
The parentÂ’s age, family composition, and the name of the reporterÂ’s supervisor
The childÂ’s age, nature, the extent of injuries, and any actions taken

8. Teen sexual activity may be achieved through coercive methods such as:
Emotional pressure
Communicating affection
Professing love
Physical force

9. Teens are most at risk for becoming victims of dating violence if they are involved with individuals who demonstrate:
Dominance, jealousy, poor anger management skills, and controlling behavior
Dominance, competition, poor communication skills
Poor communication and anger management skills
Controlling behavior and high self-esteem

10. What is a common characteristic of parents or caregivers who engage in child maltreatment?
No knowledge of child development
Unrealistic expectations of a childÂ’s emotional and physical needs
A list of prior arrests for abuse
High-income jobs

6. Offer phone numbers and resources that the woman can use when she is willing to accept assistance
7. The child's age, nature, the extent of injuries, and any actions taken
8. Physical force
9. Dominance, jealousy, poor anger management skills, and controlling behavior
10. Unrealistic expectations of a child's emotional and physical needs

11. What is a reason people who experience elder abuse may not report it?
They canÂ’t remember details
They are unsure of the definition of abuse
They fear being abandoned
They often have no way to contact anyone about the abuse

12. Which of the following is the HIGHEST priority to be addressed when a suspected victim of domestic violence plans to leave the abuser?
A safety plan
The medical record
A danger assessment
Documentation of injuries

13. What are signs that an infant has been violently shaken?
Bruising at the base of the neck
A bulging fontanel and change in the level of consciousness
A concussion and skull fracture
Subdural hematoma and retinal bleeding

14. What should the nurse record when documenting findings of abuse?
Only information regarding physical findings
Descriptions of injuries and demeanor
Details that the abused person gives permission to document
Everything the victim says

15. How should the nurse respond when an adult admits to being abused?
Ask what type of support they can offer
Call adult protective services
Tell the person to leave the abuser
Start to discuss finances

11. They fear being abandoned
12. A safety plan
13. A bulging fontanel and change in the level of consciousness
14. Descriptions of injuries and demeanor
15. Ask what type of support they can offer
